strictSummary: In Soltinho, when the leader executes a right turn, a plié is required to maintain a lower center of gravity, enhancing control.
Rising from the plié during the Soltinho right turn can be used to increase rotational speed.

drills: Practice Soltinho right turns, focusing on varying plié depth to consciously control speed and stability.
Isolate the plié and rise motion during the Soltinho right turn, alternating between controlled, low turns and faster, higher turns.
homework: Individually practice the plié and rise mechanics, observing how changes in vertical level affect rotational momentum and stability.
technicalExpansion: The principle of lowering the center of gravity (plié) increases stability and control by widening the base and engaging leg muscles for absorption, crucial for precise rotational movements. Conversely, rising reduces friction and allows for a more efficient transfer of energy into rotational momentum, increasing speed.

transcripts.transcript: No soltinho, quando o líder gira para a direita, tem que fazer um plié e ficar embaixo para ter mais controle. É só subir no caso de querer ganhar mais velocidade.
